text: The surgeon lifts the small bowel out of the pelvis, revealing a deep prolapse. The sacral uterine ligaments are visible, and the ureter is identified as the peritoneum is opened along the sacral promontory. The left iliac vein is noted nearby. The peritoneum is incised, and the cul-de-sac is redressed to reduce the prolapse. A tunnel is created behind the vagina, with the assistant lifting the vagina for better visualization. A polypropylene mesh is positioned and tailored as needed, with the anterior aspect of the rectum sutured to the mesh to prevent further intussusception. The mesh is fixed to the sacral promontory, avoiding the hypogastric nerve. An additional suture is placed to prevent higher intussusception, and the peritoneal flaps are closed over the mesh, completing the procedure.
